candidates.name: GeniusHTX/SWE-Skills-Bench
candidates.description: Public benchmark repository associated with the 2026 SWE-Skills-Bench paper, focused on isolating whether agent skills improve real-world software-engineering outcomes.
candidates.adoption_signal: GitHub repository search result id 1182696299; public; default branch main; archived=false; size 7788. Web/arXiv result links the benchmark to this repository.
candidates.complementarity: Directly complements the skills repo validation gate by testing skill marginal utility, acceptance criteria, token overhead, and regression risk.
candidates.reason: Benchmark/evaluation substrate can improve acceptance criteria without copying benchmark content; requires license and execution-surface review before any normalization.

candidates.name: microsoft/SkillOpt
candidates.description: Microsoft public repository surfaced by GitHub search for agent skills; associated public paper describes bounded add/delete/replace optimization of skill documents using scored rollouts and held-out validation.
candidates.adoption_signal: Organization-owned public repository; GitHub metadata id 1232669890; default branch main; archived=false; size 22564. Public arXiv result describes the SkillOpt method.
candidates.complementarity: Strong fit for self-improving skill doctrine: bounded edits, rejected-edit buffers, validation scores, transfer checks, and rollback discipline.
candidates.reason: High relevance to loop-engineered skill improvement, but review must check license, evaluation claims, benchmark harnesses, and whether any method can be distilled without copying or running code.

candidates.name: Narwhal-Lab/MagicSkills
candidates.description: Public organization-owned repository surfaced by GitHub search for agent skill systems and SKILL.md-style repositories.
candidates.adoption_signal: Organization-owned public repository; GitHub metadata id 1124765513; default branch main; archived=false; size 155076.
candidates.complementarity: Potential source for large-scale skill corpus structure, skill metadata conventions, and skill-package review failure modes.
candidates.reason: Large public skill repository may contain reusable corpus-management patterns, but requires license, provenance, content-safety, and redundancy review before any profile or normalization.
